tokens: Oops, there's a heat flow probe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give me heart failure after all that drilling.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I just walked too close to it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't care how close you walk to it...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I do that (pause) in training, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Grunting and breathing hard) Hey, Bob, just out of curiosity, what kind of heart rates has this drill been producing on me?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you've been running at 120 flush, Gene, with peaks of 140 to 150 from time to time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And there goes the last heat flow hole on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I tell you, if you learn how to use your instruments in this one-sixth g - you take your time and you get around - it's frankly phenomenal.
But if you try and bend over without some help; (it's) not so phenomenal.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, what a ride that Challenger gave us coming down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the (first) heat flow (probe)'s on and looking good.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me give you another one here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: While it's dirty, I'll tell you I'm in to the bottom of the white marks.
(Pause) And that's, oh, about Bravo 1 again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, the bore stem is in to the top of the white marks; I'm still putting the probe down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the top of the white marks is about Bravo 1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Pick a number you'd like to hear.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bingo, babe, you win; and it locked in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think Mark won on that one, too.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) And, Jack, I gather you are probably traipsing across the landscape with a geophone about now, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You know how big that (Geophone) rock...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I gather...You said that the LEAM was leveled and aligned, and I gather that meant it was on the black decal on top.
Do you happen to remember what a number was on that?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: But I think you know where that decal is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The little thermal shield went to F-1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And it's coming out to the south.
(Listens) I'm coming out to the south.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the (top) thermal shield is in place.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, it was until I moved it.
(Pause) (thinking about throwing the rammer) Do I need my javelin anymore?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I'll save it until after I drill the core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Probably looking up) That Sun is just bright!
I ought to put those visors down, I suppose; those other visors.
Let me take a look at my list and see whether I've got everything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to go behind a rock over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, you do want the core in a depression, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, nobody touch my heat flow.
It's the prettiest job I've ever done.
Okay, I'm going behind a boulder over here.
(Pause) Bob, I've got about 3.85 (psi) and I guess about fifty percent (oxygen).
I can't see it too well.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And no flags and no tone; and I'm on intermediate coolant and I feel great.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The Sun was...(Pause) I don't know, Jack.
I can't...I'm on about...Yeah, about 55 or 54.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, this (boulder) ought to shield that thing (the neutron probe) from the doggone (RTG)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Bob, one comment on getting the geophones within a few degrees of vertical.
In this undulating terrain (chuckles), I think they're pretty good; but it's not real easy to tell what vertical is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, this is right in line with the...(It's in a) shallow depression and it's right in line with the RTG, with a rock in the middle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As long as you're drilling behind the rock from the RTG, that's great.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That's where you're going to get it.
Let me see what I need.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, get the middle of that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I go over there, I'm not shielded, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Right in this little...It's only about a 4-meter (diameter) depression.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, you're on the other side of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I want to get back here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, all of these big boulders around here that I've looked at are the same rock type.
Who pulled over the geophone module?
That sounds like the title of a book.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hope I took (geophone) number 1 in the right direction.
Okay, number 4 will be a little hard to pick up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, all these little craters are filled with glass.
(Probably talking to the errant wrench) Come on back here.
I've got to chase this thing over the lunar surface.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) Oh, about out towards there, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As I was saying, Bob, all these big blocks that I've looked at look like the gabbroic rock that I was talking about: possibly upwards of 50 percent plagioclase rather than 30 (percent) like the mare, but an intermediate gabbro of some kind.
And one big block, there, had very-sharply defined, parallel parting planes.
I think there is a foliation of minerals that parallel that parting, but I'll have to check it out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Those parting planes (pause as he reaches the end of the geophone line) go through the whole boulder - (the boulder being) on the order of at least 3 meters long - in "outcrop".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm on my second stem (pair), here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm just about ready to pick up the biggie, geophone 4.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Geno, how are you doing?
We've been watching Jack traipse back and forth across the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, how's the visibility back to the center geophone (meaning geophone 3) (Garbled)
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You're not having to worry about extra photos yet?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Bob, my biggest problem is that the flags don't anchor.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: In general, the lines are following the contours.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know, it's the same problem you always have.
You get these threads...You get a little side force on them and, you know, with the helmet and gloves and what have you, you can't...Sometimes they go on easy; sometimes they don't.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got this one on now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, do I have a ball of spaghetti here.
But the geophones are going in the right direction.
I hope you don't have an EMI (electromagnetic interference) problem.
Can the geophone lines cross, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, if you see me start to pull over that (geophone) module there...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I mean...Oh, I won't hurt it.
It's just that it stretches the other geophones tight.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, right now we're watching Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) The anchors are completely unsuccessful - on the module, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The first core was awful loose.
I think I could have pulled it back out with my hands.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Tired) Speaking of "boy, oh boy" (meaning Jack), are you a long way off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Going to stop for a second, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've observed your problem there getting the wrench off, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I had to get down to get that third stem (pair) aligned and get it on there.
This is the easy part, but I just got myself behind the power curve for a second.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Presuming you're taking photos now on geophone 4.
Having finished geophone 4, Jack, you're about ...
right now it looks like you're about 15 minutes behind.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And no problem on the timeline so far.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (On his feet, holding the wrench) Darn it!
You know, Bob, one of the problems is I'm working in a small crater; and it's just a little difficult to work on these slopes.
I'm ready to put the drill in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get the dust out of the bit by blurping it (that is, by running the drill motor for a second).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How am I doing, Bob, on the time?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Not having heard from Bob or Jack in a while) Jack, do you read me?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, because I don't see you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm out by the big rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I hope that hole doesn't collapse.
I'm going to be awful disappointed.
(Pause) I think I could drive that heat flow flux ...
(correcting himself) or heat flow...or neutron flux in, at least for one probe, without any problem.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, if all goes well in the next few short moments, you'll have the final, unleaded (pause) core stem - automatic - in this area.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That is what it is, isn't it?
Hey, who's winning the football game?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long pause, presumably while somebody makes a phone call.
Most likely, nobody in the MOCR is watching the game.)
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay; and, Jack and Gene, the score is 10 to 10 at the half.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, would you settle for about 8 inches out of the ground?
(Breathing hard) It's about as low as I can get.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I haven't heard from them recently.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll give you A minus for that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene, having heard Bob) There he is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll go lower if I could get an A plus.
But I am going to accept an A minus, because I'll never get the wrench on it if I go any lower.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm within an inch of the white stripes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: An inch of the white stripes, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And they're worried up here that you didn't clear the flutes, Geno.
You want to tell them that so they'll be happy?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll tell them I did clear the flutes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, where are you lost on the plains.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Still discussing the clearing of the flutes) Yeah, I did.
But if you want me to do it some more, I will.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, if you cleared, that's sufficient.
And, Jack, where are you lost on the plains of Taurus-Littrow, there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Calling dramatically, like Marley's ghost in Dickens' A Christmas Carol) I'm over here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: He's 180 (degrees) from where your camera (is pointing)...from where I am.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Are you getting ready to take geophone photos or ALSEP photos?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm getting ready to enable the old geophone (as per
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I take it that means you've taken the geophone photos.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Oh, yes, sir; and I forgot the gnomon!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How about giving me a couple of quick readings here to satisfy some people.
One, was there a decal on the LEAM that you aligned it with?
There's some controversy down here that there's no decal there; and the question is, if there isn't they want a reading out of the degrees.
But we keep saying there's a LEAM decal, and we can't prove it.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And the second question is, is there a decal and was it aligned on the shade...(correcting himself) the 20-degree decal on the LSG.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You don't have to prove it to me.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Stops to listen to Bob) I was able to pull the core out with the drill, about 3 inches.
And it's all jacking material from there out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we finally got some word from the Cape to prove to people that there's a decal on the LEAM, so you don't have to go back by that, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I guess I take ALSEP photos.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Once more I tempt the fate of the god of the cables.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and, Jack, we're getting ready here to try and save a little bit of time.
And we're saying that why don't we just take two stereo pans for the ALSEP photos.
The first stereo pan will be in the vicinity of the original stereo pan; and the second one, they suggested, will be to the northwest of that original one.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yes, and I suggest that you go far enough so that you can see the LEAM past the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, Bob, (laughing) you'll be interested to know (that) I just put a plug in the top of that core; and it disappeared from sight down the center of the core.
I'll put a cap on it, too; but I want to plug it first...I want to get the rammer to plug it down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, where do you want the focus on the pan to be?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That plug was too small for the core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You've got to focus it somewhat short...Well, between 74 feet...(that is), just a little short of 74 feet?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I've already taken it at 15, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's not a calibrated detent, but I don't think you need it here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How far northwest (does Houston want the next pan)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: About the same position as the heat flow down-Suns...(Correcting himself) or, up-Suns?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I was able to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That sounds pretty good to me, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I ran that plug three-quarters...(Correcting himself) two-thirds of the way down the rammer, and it hit solid paydirt.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll put a cap on it for you, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And did you copy me, Jack, that (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, would you confirm for the ground that you got the LSP enabled?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I was on my way, and the LEAM interrupted me.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, that's cap Alpha that's on the core.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Jack, you're taking your second pan, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) I'm not sure they're hearing us all the time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Responding to Bob) Yeah, but the camera just stopped.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Breathing hard from the first strokes of the jack) Oh, man!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just said that was cap Alpha on the core.
And let me tell you, (chuckles) it's coming, but this thing is really in something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Would you believe I'm out of film, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why didn't I look at the number?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You want to give me a frame count, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Long Pause; Gene's breathing is clearly audible)
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, we're recommending magazine Hotel, and we also suggest you take the second pan, when you retake it, at 74 feet.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Man, it didn't feel like this stuff was that hard.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See if I can get this thing out of the way.
(Rests, breathing hard) See if I can get it out, is what I'm really saying.
I may be jacking the treadle down into the surface.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Jack, if you haven't put magazine Hotel on, we want to recall that and make it magazine Golf...(Correcting himself to follow Jack's lead in using women's names) Gail.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, Bob, I've already got it on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, anyway, that's black and white also.
If you want color, you want Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Stand by, Jack, if you're still at the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'm still here, but I got Hotel on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We don't have much time; otherwise I'd change it.
I should have thought of that myself.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We got a little time because I've got a lot of jacking to do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me finish the pan and come and help you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, there's not...not a lot you can do, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll get the neutron flux ready.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to get this thing out, now that I got it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, Geno, that's what you call getting down into your work.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I'll save my comments 'til later.
I hope this core is appreciated.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I have word from the back room that it is appreciated.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, that makes me feel warm.
You're going to have to bear with me.
Man, I don't know what it's in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I was afraid that would happen, with all those rocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, but it didn't go in that hard.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Geno, how about slacking off for a minute there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: One more turn and I'll get up.
I've got to hit an easy spot sooner or later.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to take a rest.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Breathing quite hard) Man, I hate to say it, but I had that 25 percent of the way there.
I can feel it (his heart) ticking now.
(Pause) I'm going cold (that is, to maximum cooling).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got your pans and a couple pictures of the heat flow probes.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you've got the two separate pans there, we're suggesting that since the CDR is still working on the core recovery, we suggest that you sample the large boulders and (also) loose material on top of some of the smaller large boulders in the vicinity.
Let's do some sampling here while Geno's pumping on the old jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You want me to help him?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Unless you guys think that two guys can do that better than one.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, you want me to spell you a little?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I don't think there's a lot you can do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Come on over here one minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I can use up some of my (cooling) water.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see if I can't get a bigger bite.
You on one end, and let me stand on the treadle and we might be able to get a bigger bite.
See, I can't get a very big bite.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, could you verify we have the LSPE Enable on (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I just hope that jack doesn't break.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Responding to Bob) No, I'll get it.
I knew there was something I needed to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get the jack end over here.
Let me put some weight here.
See what kind of bite you can get.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's what I've been doing.
See if you can get a bigger...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Marveling at the effort required) Oh, no!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Here, let me get my foot down there and you get the jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If I do it that way...Get her way down there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) See, if we can get a couple of inches at a throw, (then) we're all right.
Do that for a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me put my foot on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) It's got to loosen up sooner or later.
(Pause; Jack uses his full weight again to push the handle to the ground) Okay.
When you're tired, I'll do that and you can do this.
See, this way, you can get a bigger throw.
Let me know when and I'll do that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Does it feel like it's loosening up at all?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Oh, my UHT (is on the ground); among other things.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You want to get over here and I'll do that for a while.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can hold there, and I'll hold it, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That seems like a little easier.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That looks to me like it should be getting easy.
Just hold on to me and...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, let me get over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you come over here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we're going to get it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Come on over here and hold your foot against that thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just hold that little thing (meaning the treadle) down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I need your foot on that thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know what kind of hole we're going to have (for the neutron probe).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Get your foot down on that thing again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I jacked the treadle down about 6 inches.
(Pause as Gene does his sixth stroke of this sequence) Okay.
It's loosening up a little bit.
I keep saying that, don't I?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It changed while I had it there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you go get your pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, why don't you get your pan and your...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll get that and a few samples, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me tell you, Red Rover, let me tell you!
(Pause) I know whose face is smiling back there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You don't suppose this is why we didn't have much dust from the LM, do you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing) I saw all the way to the ground during landing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) Whoops, I moved your Central Station.
I've got to re-align on your antenna.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We should have raised the flag on this thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It (the gnomon shadow) looks just the same as when I left it, but I thought I moved it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Leave it alone for right now, Jack, and we'll get a reading on it...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) And, Jack, I guess right now, you might get some fairly rapid samples in the area, since you're probably almost ready to leave.
And can you tell us what you saw there in the vicinity; you were giving us a description of the boulders there and plate-iness and alignment of the crystals...the plag(ioclase).
You want to amplify that a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay...(Listens to Bob's final remark) I will as soon as I get back over there with a sample bag.
Bag 10 Echo - one-zero Echo - is a sample of the very large boulder (Geophone Rock) that's just beyond Geophone 3.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Copy that, one-zero Echo, and boulder east of which geophone?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And I got a few photos to document the boulder.
I'm not sure I documented the sample, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's the same kind of rock I saw near the LM.
I'm beginning to lean towards 50 percent plagioclase, though.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I had to remove the treadle from the hole and I'll tell you later why.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Responding to Bob) No, I'll tell you later why.
I'm just figuring, oh me, how am I going to get all this stuff now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to lose my hole.
In our fiasco over here, we knocked everything over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Nope, I've just got to stoop over to get things and that's a major, major effort these days.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got a delicate core in one hand, and I'm trying to get some core caps in the other.
(To Bob) You'll be glad to know it's full (at the bit end), Bob.
And while I'm the only one to see the bottom end right now, I'm going to tell you (that) it looks like what I'm walking on, but it's obviously not powdery.
It's obviously very cohesive, because the bottom of the core is not smooth.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm being very careful with your core here, but I've got to do a few little housekeeping chores first.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Have you got that neutron flux over there in the vicinity, or is it still back at the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, sir, I already got it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And Jack, in your travels there, while you're doing some sampling, if you happen to wander by in the approximate vicinity of the deep core, you might get us a Rover sample of the soil there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, the core is filled to within an eighth or certainly less than a quarter of an inch from the bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Sounds like a good candidate for a cap.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yes, sir, and it's got Bravo on and the plug has been discarded.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, let me see what else I can get here, before I get too upset.
I need my...The drill, besides performing admirably, is a tool of necessity to lean over and pick things up with.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Except when you let it fall down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And our next priority is to put the neutron flux down the hole, we hope.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Man, I don't even know if I can find the hole.
I guess I can see it down there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You asked, and with a little bit of luck, you shall receive.
(Pause) Listen, I'm earning my three and a quarter a day today.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) Oh, boy, I don't want to lose the rammer either.
Let me get that before that gets lost in the shuffle.
We don't want to lose that, for sure.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) I bet you all think I'm stepping on that hole, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, I see no clear alignment of plagioclase or pyroxene in this rock.
That's the one with the parting in it.

It looks as if - integrating what I've seen here and over at the big rock, the geophone rock - the layering or the foliation or the parting, whichever it is, is the result of variations in vesicle concentrations.
The sample 10 Echo is a sample of the more coarsely vesicular rock.
I could not get one of the more finely or non-vesicular fragments.
But I got pictures of it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm having trouble getting...(Listens to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, do you see any evidence of soil on top of some of these medium-sized boulders?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: A little bit of dust in some of the holes.
But there's not enough to sample at this point.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Perhaps to someone in Houston) He's picking up (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Vesicle walls do not seem to be as shiny (as in other Apollo lavas)...Most of them seem to have dust in them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The vesicles are not cleanly (that is, smoothly) spherical.
They're spherical but they have fairly rough outlines.
They look as if there's been some recrystallization.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I will verify that the lower section is On (that is, activated).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I picked the wrong rock to sample with a scoop, I'll tell you that.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you, housekeeping is the key to the world right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, another key to the world is whether the...(Stops and listens)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, make sure that the top of it doesn't go down through the hole, too, and disappear, either by putting it through the treadle, or if you're sure that the...Or whatever.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, Bob, that sure is a good thought.
You know, I had to take the treadle off because the jack (handle) wouldn't go down and there's no way I could put that treadle...Well, let me turn (the top section) on first.
It may go down that hole.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How big's the hole look, Geno?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: See, the jack wouldn't...(Listens) Well, looks big enough to put this down.
Let me use my judgment on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) I verified the top (section) was On (that is, activated), by the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Excited) See what happened, here, to that treadle, Bob?
I couldn't get the jack (handle) to go down and it made the hole oblong when I...But it's all right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I mean, it ended up all right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And why don't we get you two guys together again, now, and break down the core and press on.
And we've got a little revision here to the EVA.
I'll get with you in just minute, as soon as I find out what it is.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I feel pretty good about that.
That makes me feel pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Sample) bag 174...(correcting himself) 474 (is) soil from next to this big rock.
I can't get a chunk of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Missing Jack's correction) 174, fillet beside the big rock.
And, Jack, while you coming back here to the Rover, why don't you get one more Rover sample in the vicinity of the deep drill, while you and Gene get ready to take on the core stems.
And because of being a little bit behind here, what we're doing is, we're getting prepared to drop Station 1 in favor of doing Steno (Crater).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And I'll get with you on more details on that in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, how far behind are we?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And part of the problem is that we're a little short on oxygen on Gene's PLSS.

It looks like it's a 6 hours and 45 minute EVA from that point of view (rather than seven hours as planned), which means that we'd have to leave Station 1 too early (to get very much useful work done).
(Pause) Which is the reason to curtail Station 1, apart from just (being) behind which is what the hooker was.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm approaching the rear of the Rover.
I've got the core, the cap, the wrench, and the rammer.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I didn't mean to breathe up all that oxygen.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there's some things you can't help.
Even the (Flight) Surgeon agrees with me on that one.
And for your thinking, Jack and Gene, what we're doing is planning on going to the west side of Steno and that boulder field that's part way out to Station 1.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Gene) Okay, you want to break that (core) and I'll go get this sample, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah; I'll break this, Jack; no sweat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gene has pretty well chewed up the ground.
I helped him (chew up the soil).
Do you want me to get a little ways away from it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't think we're interested in a surface sample in the last top little bit, I think.
It's just the top...Just a surface sample.
(Pause) Anything there in the dirt, Jack.
It doesn't have to be a skim sample of any sort.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm breaking down the core at the tail end of the Rover, here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, don't do it yet, I haven't gotten it broken down yet (without spilling anything).
(Pause) But I got it out of the ground with a little help.
(Pause) Okay, first piece of three sections.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I have to tell you which end I'm taking it from.
I don't remember which end I've got here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's all right, since we got the cap and Alpha on one end and Bravo on the other end.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: There's a cap that's going to be tough to get on.
I put that on with a hammer.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, there's a mixture of soil and a rock in (sample bag) 475.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The soil came from about 0 to 5 centimeters (depth).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And it's about 3 meters from the hole.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, cap Charlie is opposite Alpha.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, it's about 3 meters from the hole.
I got a stereo "before" at 11 feet and one "after" at 11 feet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And how about a frame count there, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Let me configure the old LRV sampler, here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, boy; oh, boy; oh, boy; oh, boy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When you took those two pans at the ALSEP, was one at 15 feet and one at 20 feet?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: One was at...Focus was at 15 and 74.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a partial pan on mag A (Alpha or 147), which was taken at 15 (feet focus).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob...I can't see what it is.
I guess Delta and Echo is the 2-section core.
Delta being adjacent to the first section of 3.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, baby, just go on there nice.
(Pause; Gene hammers the cap with his fist) And it's on tight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to take a big
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We got them three cores; we got the neutron flux down; and we got two heat probes, and an ALSEP.
I don't care if we are 30 minutes late.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, did I give you the last cap?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The way they're broken down, there's no problem.
The 3-2-3 stands out and the Bravo on the bit end.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Confirming what Jack wants him to do) Pull this off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) No, no, no, just the...The total thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I have to line it up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me give you a (gravimeter) reading, Bob, before you speak.
Wait a minute, let me get it over with.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did you punch Grav a second time?
That's identical to the first one.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, but did you punch Grav after the first reading you gave me there at the ALSEP?
Or are you just reading me the same measurements you did before?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I called them out every time.
(Listens) Bob, I'm reading it right here.
Everywhere I punched Grav, you've got it written down somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, and I didn't copy your punching Grav, but the one...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: When I went to get the treadle and the neutron flux and rammer, I did not punch Grav.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So that's the same as the first one.
And guys, we're ready for you guys, as you go along here, to do the Geo Prep and press on.
As I say, we'll go to Steno and come back from there and do the S.E.P.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We'd also like to know if you have the gnomon, back at the Rover?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, we're just...(Listens to Bob's final question)
transcript.speaker: Cernan
